models.generation_context: All-new third-generation (C 174), launched for 2026 model year as the first vehicle on Mercedes' MMA (Mercedes Modular Architecture) platform; built around 800V EV architecture with future 48V mild-hybrid ICE variants planned
models.model_summary: The all-new 2026 CLA is the first Mercedes-Benz built on the MMA platform and the first to offer 'EQ Technology' as a sub-brand replacing the EQ nomenclature. The US-market lineup launches as two pure-electric trims — the 268-hp single-motor CLA 250+ (374 EPA-rated miles of range, the longest of any Mercedes EV) and the 349-hp dual-motor CLA 350 4MATIC. The CLA introduces an 85-kWh silicon-anode lithium-ion battery, native 320-kW DC fast charging via NACS, an 11-kW onboard AC charger, and Mercedes' new MB.OS chip-to-cloud architecture with up to three 14-inch screens.

reliability.summary: JD Power 2026 US VDS rates the mercedes-benz brand at 235 PP100 (lower is better) — worse than the 204 industry average and worse than the 217 premium-segment average. Consumer Reports has not published a 2026 model-specific score; the model inherits the Audi/BMW/Mercedes brand reliability picture (below average (brand fallback), est. 2/5). Sourced from the published CR 2026 Brand Report Card and JD Power 2026 VDS brand ranking.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has NOT yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (the US APEAL Study typically releases in July). No 2026 model-specific APEAL score is available for this model. The 2025 APEAL score may be applicable as carryover but has not been adopted here pending the 2026 release.

professional_reviews.summary: Early reviews praise the new CLA's class-leading EPA range, hyper-efficient 800V architecture, refined ride and futuristic three-screen MBUX Superscreen interior. Critics note tight rear-seat legroom (33.6 in.), a steeply-raked roofline that limits headroom for tall passengers, and a 14.3 cu-ft trunk that is competitive but unexceptional. The CLA was named European Car of the Year 2026.

owner_reviews.summary: Too new for meaningful owner-review data; CLA deliveries to US customers began in early 2026.

EPA range of 374 mi is achieved with 18" Aero wheels (R22 spec); choosing larger wheels reduces EPA range to ~317 mi and combined MPGe to 109. Curb weight ~4,530 lb. The CLA 250+ is the longest-range EV in the Mercedes-Benz US lineup.

models.notes: First Mercedes-Benz on the new MMA (Mercedes Modular Architecture) platform; first to use 'with EQ Technology' nomenclature in place of the prior 'EQ' sub-brand. 2026 US launch is BEV-only (CLA 250+ and CLA 350 4MATIC); a 48V mild-hybrid ICE variant has been announced for global markets but not confirmed for US 2026 MY. Both trims share the 85 kWh silicon-anode battery, 800V architecture, 320 kW DC fast charging via NACS native plug, and 11 kW AC charging. Standard ADAS suite is comprehensive (AEB, lane keep, ACC, blind spot, etc.); NHTSA and IIHS have not yet rated the new CLA — set to null. The CLA was named European Car of the Year 2026.

models.generation_context: Fifth-generation W206 platform, introduced for the 2022 model year; carried into 2026 with minor equipment updates. AMG C 63 S E Performance variant introduced for 2024 MY as the first 4-cylinder PHEV in the C 63 nameplate's history.
models.model_summary: The 2026 Mercedes-Benz C-Class Sedan is a compact luxury sedan offered with two distinct powertrain lines: a 255-hp 2.0L turbo-4 with 48V mild-hybrid (C 300 RWD and C 300 4MATIC), and a high-performance line that includes the 416-hp AMG C 43 (also 2.0L turbo-4 mild-hybrid with electric exhaust-gas turbocharger) and the 671-hp AMG C 63 S E Performance plug-in hybrid built around a handcrafted 2.0L turbo-4 and a 150 kW rear-axle electric motor. All trims share the W206 chassis with a 112.8-in wheelbase, MBUX with vertical 11.9-in touchscreen and 12.3-in digital cluster, and 4MATIC AWD on every variant except the base C 300.

reliability.summary: Consumer Reports projects about-average reliability for the 2026 C-Class based on limited data from the 2023-2025 model years and Mercedes-Benz brand-wide scores. KBB owner reliability rating ~2.9/5. JD Power has not published a 2026-MY VDS score.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power has not published a 2026-MY APEAL score for the C-Class. Owner sentiment on Edmunds is generally positive (4.0/5) with praise for cabin luxury and ride refinement.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently praise the C-Class's luxurious cabin, refined ride quality, and standard 11.9-in MBUX touchscreen, while noting that the 2.0L turbo-4 in the C 300 (255 hp) feels adequate rather than effortless for a luxury sedan in this price bracket. The AMG C 43 is admired for its 416-hp output and 4MATIC traction but is criticized by some for losing the iconic V8 soundtrack of prior C 63 generations, while the new AMG C 63 S E Performance PHEV delivers supercar-rivaling 671-hp output and 3.3-sec 0-60 numbers at the cost of curb weight (4,817 lb) and a small 6.1-kWh battery yielding only ~3 mi of EPA electric range.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise the luxurious cabin, smooth ride, advanced tech and good fuel economy from the mild-hybrid C 300. Common complaints involve MBUX infotainment glitches/lag, interior rattles, and reliability concerns rated about average rather than premium-brand exceptional. KBB owner reliability sub-score is ~2.9/5.

EPA-rated electric range is only 3 miles; the system is configured for performance and weight balance, not EV range. 2026 fuel-economy figures shown reference the latest available fueleconomy.gov entry (carried from 2025 MY id=49018) as the 2026 entry was not yet visible on the EPA portal as of May 2026; manufacturer-published MPGe figures (37 combined) and 20 mpg gas-mode combined are unchanged year-over-year. The PHEV battery uses a 10yr/155k warranty consistent with Mercedes 2026 PHEV/EV practice. NHTSA has not yet rated the 2026 C-Class; IIHS Top Safety Pick applies to the C-Class 4-door sedan and is assumed to extend to the C 63 variant (same body shell). fueleconomy.gov did not yet have a specific 2026 entry for this trim as of 2026-05-12; source set to model browse page until EPA publishes.

models.notes: Two powertrain lines per multi-powertrain rule: (1) ICE/48V mild-hybrid line — C 300 (RWD, base), C 300 4MATIC, AMG C 43 4MATIC; (2) PHEV line — AMG C 63 S E Performance (sole trim, marked is_base_trim: true). models.generation_context: First-generation CLE (C 236), introduced for 2024 model year as a consolidated two-door replacement for the previous C-Class Coupe and E-Class Coupe; rides on Mercedes' MRA II rear-drive platform
models.model_summary: The Mercedes-Benz CLE Coupe consolidates the outgoing C-Class and E-Class coupes into a single two-door line, sized between them on a stretched C-Class chassis. The 2026 US lineup includes two MBUSA-branded trims (CLE 300 4MATIC and CLE 450 4MATIC) plus the AMG-tuned CLE 53 4MATIC+; all three pair a turbocharged engine with Mercedes' 48-volt integrated starter-generator mild-hybrid system that can briefly add ~23 hp during acceleration. All trims are standard with 4MATIC all-wheel drive in the US market.

reliability.summary: JD Power 2026 US VDS rates the mercedes-benz brand at 235 PP100 (lower is better) — worse than the 204 industry average and worse than the 217 premium-segment average. Consumer Reports has not published a 2026 model-specific score; the model inherits the Audi/BMW/Mercedes brand reliability picture (below average (brand fallback), est. 2/5). Sourced from the published CR 2026 Brand Report Card and JD Power 2026 VDS brand ranking.

No 2026 model-specific APEAL score is available for this model. The 2025 APEAL score may be applicable as carryover but has not been adopted here pending the 2026 release.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently praise the CLE Coupe's elegant proportions, refined ride quality and rich interior with MBUX dual-screen tech. The inline-6 CLE 450 is highlighted as the sweet spot for smooth, effortless thrust, while the AMG CLE 53 earns nods for added composure and an electric exhaust turbocharger but is criticized for feeling more grand-tourer than razor-edged AMG. Common gripes include cramped rear seats, modest trunk volume for the size, and an options sheet that pushes typically-equipped cars well past base MSRP.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner-review data for the 2026 model year is sparse; the CLE generation overall draws praise for build quality, ride comfort and engine refinement, with complaints about tight rear-seat access and pricey options packages.

models.generation_context: First-generation A236 platform, introduced for the 2024 model year as a single replacement for the prior C-Class Cabriolet and E-Class Cabriolet. Continued for 2026 with minor equipment updates; AMG CLE 53 Cabriolet variant available as the performance halo.
models.model_summary: The 2026 Mercedes-Benz CLE Cabriolet is a 2+2 four-seat luxury soft-top convertible that consolidates the former C-Class Cabriolet and E-Class Cabriolet into a single model line. The US lineup comprises the CLE 300 4MATIC (255-hp 2.0L turbo-4 with 48V mild hybrid), CLE 450 4MATIC (375-hp 3.0L turbo I6 with 48V mild hybrid), and the AMG CLE 53 4MATIC+ (443-hp twin-scroll turbo I6 with 48V mild hybrid). All CLE Cabriolets ride on the A236 platform and feature a fabric Acoustic Soft Top that opens or closes in approximately 20 seconds at speeds up to 37 mph, plus Mercedes' AIRCAP automatic wind deflector and AIRSCARF neck-warmer system.

reliability.summary: JD Power 2026 US VDS rates the mercedes-benz brand at 235 PP100 (lower is better) — worse than the 204 industry average and worse than the 217 premium-segment average. Consumer Reports has not published a 2026 model-specific score; the model inherits the Audi/BMW/Mercedes brand reliability picture (below average (brand fallback), est. 2/5). Sourced from the published CR 2026 Brand Report Card and JD Power 2026 VDS brand ranking.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has NOT yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (the US APEAL Study typically releases in July). No 2026 model-specific APEAL score is available for this model. The 2025 APEAL score may be applicable as carryover but has not been adopted here pending the 2026 release.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the CLE Cabriolet's polished open-air refinement, well-isolated cabin with the Acoustic Top raised, and the smooth straight-six in the CLE 450 and AMG CLE 53. The CLE 300's 2.0L turbo-4 is considered adequate but not exhilarating for the curb weight, and critics consistently note that trunk space drops sharply with the top stowed and that the rear seats are best treated as occasional. The optional AIRCAP and AIRSCARF make top-down driving comfortable in cooler weather.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner-review data is limited because the CLE nameplate is only in its third model year. Early owner sentiment on Edmunds and KBB skews positive on style and refinement but raises concerns over MBUX glitches and dealer-service experiences.

models.notes: The CLE Cabriolet (A236) replaces the prior C-Class Cabriolet and E-Class Cabriolet in Mercedes' US lineup, mirroring the CLE Coupe consolidation. All three trims share the fabric Acoustic Soft Top (operable up to 37 mph), AIRCAP automatic wind deflector, AIRSCARF neck-warmer headrests, MBUX with 11.9-in portrait touchscreen and 12.3-in digital cluster, and 4-seat (2+2) capacity. Powertrain classification: per project rules, all three trims use 'ice' with explicit '48V MHEV' notation in the transmission text and notable_other bullets, rather than the 'hybrid' classification used for mild-hybrid powertrains in some earlier Mercedes entries. Cargo follows the sedan/coupe pattern with only trunk_cuft populated; trunk volume drops from ~9.6 cu ft (top up) to ~6.0 cu ft (top stowed). NHTSA and IIHS do not publish ratings for convertibles, so safety crash ratings are null across all trims, and IIHS Top Safety Pick designations are not available for convertible body styles. Adaptive cruise control and rear automatic braking are part of the optional Driver Assistance Package across all trims and are marked false on the base trim's standard_adas. Image entries point to Mercedes-Benz USA consumer pages flagged needs_scraping:true; build phase will resolve direct CDN asset URLs from the page HTML.

models.generation_context: Sixth-generation W214 platform, launched for the 2024 model year and carried into 2026 with minor equipment updates. The AMG E 53 Hybrid 4MATIC+ was introduced for 2025 MY as the first plug-in hybrid in the E 53 nameplate's history, pairing the AMG inline-6 with a rear-axle electric motor and a 28.6 kWh high-performance battery.
models.model_summary: The 2026 Mercedes-Benz E-Class Sedan is a midsize luxury sedan offered with three powertrain configurations: a 255-hp 2.0L turbo-4 with 48V mild-hybrid (E 350 RWD and E 350 4MATIC), a 375-hp 3.0L turbo inline-6 with 48V mild-hybrid (E 450 4MATIC), and the high-performance AMG E 53 Hybrid 4MATIC+ plug-in hybrid combining a handcrafted 3.0L turbo inline-6 with a rear-axle electric motor for 577 hp combined. All trims share the W214 chassis with a 116.7-in wheelbase, MBUX Superscreen with a 14.4-in central touchscreen and 12.3-in digital cluster, standard panoramic roof, and a comprehensive standard ADAS suite. The AMG E 53 PHEV offers ~21 miles of EPA electric-only range from its 28.6 kWh battery.

reliability.summary: Consumer Reports projects about-average predicted reliability for the 2026 E-Class based on a few model years of W214 data and brand-wide averages. JD Power has not published a 2026-MY VDS score. Historical Mercedes E-Class reliability has been mid-pack for the luxury midsize segment.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power has not published a 2026-MY APEAL score for the E-Class. Owner sentiment on Edmunds and KBB remains strong, with broad praise for cabin tech and ride refinement.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently rate the 2026 E-Class among the best midsize luxury sedans, praising its hushed cabin, plush ride on the optional AIRMATIC air suspension, and class-leading MBUX Superscreen interior. The 2.0L turbo-4 E 350 is described as adequate but uninspiring relative to the silken 3.0L turbo inline-6 in the E 450, which is the powertrain most testers recommend. The AMG E 53 Hybrid is praised for its 577-hp PHEV powertrain and 3.6-sec 0-60 but criticized for its 5,000+ lb curb weight and modest 21-mi EV range relative to mainstream PHEVs.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise the E-Class's interior luxury, ride comfort, technology and engine refinement (particularly the I6 in the E 450). Common complaints focus on the steep options pricing, occasional MBUX touchscreen lag and excessive use of capacitive controls on the steering wheel.

The Wagon body returns to the US only in AMG E 53 HYBRID PHEV form.
models.model_summary: The 2026 Mercedes-AMG E 53 HYBRID 4MATIC+ Wagon is the only E-Class Wagon offered in the US for 2026 and the brand's first AMG performance wagon sold here in years. It pairs a 3.0L turbocharged inline-6 with a rear-axle-mounted electric motor and a 28.6 kWh plug-in hybrid battery for 577 combined horsepower (603 hp with Race Start), roughly 21 miles of EV-only range, and 0-60 mph in about 3.7 seconds. Mercedes confirmed the model in 2024 as an expansion of the US lineup, joining the AMG E 53 HYBRID Sedan.

reliability.summary: JD Power 2026 US VDS rates the mercedes-benz brand at 235 PP100 (lower is better) — worse than the 204 industry average and worse than the 217 premium-segment average. Consumer Reports 2026 predicts below average (E-Class platform) reliability for this model (2/5 on CR's scale). Cross-checked against published CR 2026 reliability page.

professional_reviews.summary: Early reviews are largely positive, framing the AMG E 53 Wagon as a unique long-roof PHEV that combines genuine performance (sub-4-second 0-60), 21 miles of usable EV-only range, and S-Class-level interior tech. Critics flag a steep ~$113K starting price for a wagon, added curb weight from the battery pack reducing handling sharpness vs the prior E 63 Wagon, and limited cargo room relative to mainstream luxury SUVs. The model has been called the spiritual successor to the discontinued E 63 S Wagon, even if it trails the old V8 on outright power.

owner_reviews.summary: Too new for meaningful owner-review data; US deliveries of the AMG E 53 HYBRID Wagon began in early 2026 and sample sizes on Edmunds and KBB are not yet meaningful.

models.model_summary: The 2026 Mercedes-Benz S-Class remains the flagship full-size luxury sedan benchmark, offered in the US in four powertrain configurations: the S 500 4MATIC inline-six MHEV, the S 580 4MATIC biturbo V8 MHEV, the S 580e 4MATIC plug-in hybrid with roughly 46 miles of EV range, and the AMG S 63 E Performance plug-in hybrid producing 791 hp combined. All trims share the W223 long-wheelbase chassis, MBUX with dual 12.8-inch OLED screens, standard rear-axle steering, and AIRMATIC air suspension. The S 580e is the volume PHEV; the AMG S 63 E Performance is the most powerful S-Class ever produced.

reliability.summary: Current-generation W223 S-Class has received mixed reliability marks from Consumer Reports, which cites concerns with in-car electronics, complex air suspension, and 48V MHEV system glitches. JD Power has not published a 2026 VDS specific to the S-Class.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power has not published a 2026 APEAL score specific to the S-Class; the 2024 APEAL study ranked the S-Class highly within the Large Premium Car segment, reflecting strong owner emotional attachment to the design, interior quality and technology.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers continue to position the S-Class as the gold standard of full-size luxury sedans for ride quality, rear-seat comfort, and isolation, with the S 580e plug-in hybrid praised for delivering near-silent EV commutes alongside V8-rivaling combined output. Critics consistently flag the touch-heavy MBUX interface for over-reliance on the center screen and call out the AMG S 63 E Performance's high curb weight (5,400+ lb) and stratospheric price as the points where the formula begins to strain. Across powertrains, the S-Class is regarded as the most refined long-distance luxury sedan still in production.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise the unmatched ride comfort, hushed cabin, rear-seat amenities, and powertrain refinement. Common complaints concern the steep depreciation, the complexity of the MBUX touch interface, and occasional electronic gremlins (window switch panels, ambient lighting, MBUX restarts).

models.model_summary: The 2026 Mercedes-Maybach S-Class is the long-wheelbase, top-of-the-line ultra-luxury sedan derived from the W/Z223 S-Class, featuring roughly 7 inches of additional rear legroom over the standard long-wheelbase S-Class. The two-trim lineup pairs a 496-hp 4.0L biturbo V8 with a 48-volt mild-hybrid system in the S 580 4MATIC, and a handcrafted 621-hp 6.0L biturbo V12 (one of the last V12s on sale in any segment) in the range-topping S 680 4MATIC. The 2026 model year brings a facelift with revised exterior styling, MB.OS-based MBUX, an available Hyperscreen-style display layout and an AI-enhanced voice assistant.

reliability.summary: JD Power 2026 US VDS rates the mercedes-benz brand at 235 PP100 (lower is better) — worse than the 204 industry average and worse than the 217 premium-segment average. Consumer Reports has not published a 2026 model-specific score; the model inherits the Audi/BMW/Mercedes brand reliability picture (below average (S-Class based), est. 2/5). Sourced from the published CR 2026 Brand Report Card and JD Power 2026 VDS brand ranking.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has NOT yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (the US APEAL Study typically releases in July). No 2026 model-specific APEAL score is available for this model. The 2025 APEAL score may be applicable as carryover but has not been adopted here pending the 2026 release.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers describe the 2026 Maybach S-Class as effectively the benchmark for chauffeured rear-seat luxury under $250k, praising the cosseting ride quality from E-Active Body Control, the silence of the cabin, the rear executive lounge with massage and footrests, and the Burmester High-End 4D 31-speaker audio system. Critics note the trunk is small for the segment (12.3-12.9 cu ft), the V12 S 680 is thirsty enough to warrant a meaningful gas-guzzler tax, and that competitive rear-seat technology from the BMW 7-Series and Bentley Flying Spur has narrowed the gap. The MY2026 facelift modernizes the dashboard tech but the underlying platform is now five years old.

owner_reviews.summary: No meaningful 2026 owner-review aggregation is available yet. Owner feedback on prior-year Maybach S-Class models on Edmunds and KBB is generally very positive on comfort and prestige but flags software glitches and high running costs.

models.model_summary: The 2026 Mercedes-AMG GT Coupe is the 2-door front-mid-engine grand tourer flagship of the AMG lineup, sharing its platform with the SL roadster but adding a fixed roof, 2+2 optional seating, and standard AMG Performance 4MATIC+ all-wheel drive on V8 trims. The 4.0L biturbo V8 powertrain spans the 469-hp GT 55, the 577-hp GT 63, and the track-focused 603-hp GT 63 PRO; at the top of the range, the GT 63 S E PERFORMANCE plug-in hybrid pairs the V8 with a 150-kW rear electric motor and a 6.1-kWh high-performance battery for a combined 805 hp and 1,047 lb-ft. The C192 brings standard all-wheel drive, rear-wheel steering, AMG ACTIVE RIDE CONTROL active suspension, and a more usable cabin than the C190 it replaces.

reliability.summary: JD Power 2026 US VDS rates the mercedes-benz brand at 235 PP100 (lower is better) — worse than the 204 industry average and worse than the 217 premium-segment average. Consumer Reports has not published a 2026 model-specific score; the model inherits the Audi/BMW/Mercedes brand reliability picture (below average (brand fallback), est. 2/5). Sourced from the published CR 2026 Brand Report Card and JD Power 2026 VDS brand ranking.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has NOT yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (the US APEAL Study typically releases in July). No 2026 model-specific APEAL score is available for this model. The 2025 APEAL score may be applicable as carryover but has not been adopted here pending the 2026 release.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the C192 GT Coupe for its handcrafted AMG biturbo V8 character, standard 4MATIC+ traction, AMG ACTIVE RIDE CONTROL hydraulic suspension that delivers both flat cornering and surprisingly compliant ride quality, and the addition of optional 2+2 rear seats that make it more livable than the prior generation. Common criticisms include weight gain over the C190 predecessor, tight rear seats best reserved for cargo, polarizing piped-in engine sound, and pricing that climbs steeply through the GT 63 PRO and GT 63 S E PERFORMANCE PHEV. The 805-hp PHEV is hailed as a straight-line monster (2.7 sec 0-60) but criticized for its 1-mile EPA electric range and heft.

owner_reviews.summary: Edmunds and KBB show very small owner-review sample sizes for the C192 GT Coupe (low double digits combined across model years), insufficient for a meaningful star rating. Owners cite straight-line performance, AMG drivetrain refinement and standout styling as positives; complaints center on long-distance comfort, fuel economy, and infotainment learning curve.

models.generation_context: X290 generation, introduced for 2019 model year and refreshed in 2024; sedan-style four-door coupe sharing its platform with the E-Class but engineered by AMG in Affalterbach
models.model_summary: The Mercedes-AMG GT 4-Door Coupe is a high-performance four-door fastback that combines sedan practicality with two-door AMG GT styling. For 2026 the US lineup runs from the inline-six GT 43 to the 791-hp GT 63 S E Performance plug-in hybrid hypersedan that uses Formula 1-derived hybrid technology. All variants offer AMG Performance 4MATIC+ all-wheel drive (the GT 43 is RWD-biased AWD), AMG SPEEDSHIFT MCT 9G transmissions, and rear-wheel-steering on higher trims.

reliability.summary: JD Power 2026 US VDS rates the mercedes-benz brand at 235 PP100 (lower is better) — worse than the 204 industry average and worse than the 217 premium-segment average. Consumer Reports has not published a 2026 model-specific score; the model inherits the Audi/BMW/Mercedes brand reliability picture (below average (brand fallback), est. 2/5). Sourced from the published CR 2026 Brand Report Card and JD Power 2026 VDS brand ranking.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently praise the GT 4-Door's broad performance bandwidth — from the smooth turbocharged inline-six in the GT 43 to the savage 791-hp hybrid powertrain of the GT 63 S E Performance — along with its rich interior, AMG Track Pace telemetry, and genuine four-seat usability. Common criticisms include a tight rear seat under the fastback roof, a polarizing exterior compared with the conventional E-Class, and pricing that climbs steeply above $200,000 with options on the top trim. The GT 63 S E Performance is regarded as one of the fastest production sedans ever sold.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner-review sample sizes are very small for the AMG GT 4-Door Coupe in the 2026 MY; aggregated scores are not yet meaningful. Owners broadly praise drivetrain refinement and luxury, with most complaints centered on ride harshness in Sport+ modes and high running costs.

models.generation_context: R232 generation, introduced for 2022 MY; first SL fully developed by Mercedes-AMG, returning to a fabric soft top with a 2+2 cabin and standard AWD (4MATIC+) on V8 trims after decades of metal-folding-roof SLs
models.model_summary: The 2026 Mercedes-AMG SL is the R232-generation roadster developed entirely by Mercedes-AMG, swapping the previous metal-folding hardtop for a Z-fold fabric soft top and a 2+2 cabin. The US lineup spans four AMG trims plus a top-tier Mercedes-Maybach SL 680 Monogram Series: the 416-hp turbo-four SL 43 (RWD); the 469-hp V8 SL 55 4MATIC+; the 577-hp V8 SL 63 4MATIC+; the 805-hp V8 plug-in hybrid SL 63 S E Performance; and the 577-hp Maybach SL 680 Monogram Series, the first two-seat Maybach. All ride on AMG's aluminum-intensive composite spaceframe with multi-link suspension.

reliability.summary: JD Power 2026 US VDS rates the mercedes-benz brand at 235 PP100 (lower is better) — worse than the 204 industry average and worse than the 217 premium-segment average. Consumer Reports has not published a 2026 model-specific score; the model inherits the Audi/BMW/Mercedes brand reliability picture (below average (brand fallback), est. 2/5). Sourced from the published CR 2026 Brand Report Card and JD Power 2026 VDS brand ranking.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently praise the R232 SL for combining genuine sporting capability with grand-touring comfort, with every trim delivering confident acceleration and cornering. The V8 trims (SL 55 and SL 63) draw the strongest recommendations for blending sound, performance and usability, while the 805-hp SL 63 S E Performance PHEV is described as monumentally fast but heavy. Critics call out a small 7.5 cu-ft trunk, fiddly touchscreen-led controls, and tight rear 2+2 seats best treated as occasional or cargo space. The Maybach SL 680 Monogram Series is positioned as a fashion-forward grand tourer rather than an outright sports car.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner-review aggregators do not report enough 2026 SL owner reviews to publish a star rating; the R232 SL's low sales volume keeps individual MY sample sizes too small. Owners of earlier R232 model years generally praise the styling, V8 character and AMG-developed chassis, while complaints commonly cite small trunk space, touchscreen ergonomics and ride firmness on 21-inch wheels.

models.notes: R232-generation SL Roadster, the first SL developed entirely by Mercedes-AMG. The 2026 US lineup contains five trims across two powertrain lines: an ICE line of four trims (AMG SL 43 base, SL 55 4MATIC+, SL 63 4MATIC+, and the ultra-luxury two-seat Mercedes-Maybach SL 680 Monogram Series), plus a sole PHEV line consisting of the AMG SL 63 S E Performance. The Maybach SL 680 is included as the top-of-range step-up of the ICE line (sole Maybach offering on this body), while the SL 63 S E Performance is the sole PHEV trim and is its own line's base per the project's sole-trim base rule. The SL 43 was originally launched at 375 hp / 354 lb-ft (M139 turbo-I4 with F1-derived electric exhaust-gas turbocharger and 48V mild-hybrid); for MY2026 Mercedes-Benz USA's spec sheet states 416 hp / 369 lb-ft, which is used here. SL 43 is the only SL trim that is RWD-only — all V8 trims are 4MATIC+. R232 is a 2+2 (4-seat) car except the Maybach SL 680, which is a strict two-seater. NHTSA and IIHS have not crash-tested any R232 SL, so safety ratings are null across all trims. EPA combined ratings for the SL 55, SL 63 and Maybach are approximate (per EPA Fuel Economy Guide rounding from city/highway). All image URLs point to MBUSA model-page URLs and are flagged needs_scraping: true for follow-up asset extraction during the build phase.

models.generation_context: Second-generation GLA (H247), introduced for 2021 MY; mid-cycle refresh for 2024 brought updated styling and dual 10.25" MBUX displays. The 2026 MY adds subtle styling tweaks, new appearance packages (including a Midnight Edition with 20" AMG wheels), retuned suspension, and an upgraded MBUX system. AMG GLA 35 performance variant is treated as a separate model per project conventions.
models.model_summary: The 2026 GLA SUV is Mercedes-Benz's entry-level subcompact luxury crossover, sharing its FWD-based platform with the CLA (outgoing gen), GLB and A-Class. Both US trims use the same 221 hp / 258 lb-ft 2.0L turbo I4 with 48V mild-hybrid assist, paired to an 8-speed dual-clutch automatic, with the choice of FWD (GLA 250) or 4MATIC AWD (GLA 250 4MATIC). The cabin features dual 10.25" displays running the latest MBUX, wireless Apple CarPlay/Android Auto, and a comprehensive standard ADAS suite; the panoramic roof, Burmester audio and adaptive cruise control remain optional packages.

reliability.summary: Consumer Reports expects the 2026 GLA to be less reliable than the average new car, based on data from similar Mercedes-Benz SUV models; JD Power has not published a 2026 VDS score for the GLA at the time of research.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has NOT yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (the US APEAL Study typically releases in July). No 2026 model-specific APEAL score is available for this model. The 2025 APEAL score may be applicable as carryover but has not been adopted here pending the 2026 release.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently praise the GLA's upscale cabin, refined dual-screen MBUX tech, strong fit-and-finish and the well-judged 221 hp turbo engine, which is described as more than enough for the vehicle's size. Common criticisms are a stiff, choppy ride (especially on larger wheels), elevated road noise, modest rear-seat space and the fact that many expected features (panoramic roof, adaptive cruise, premium audio) require costly option packages. Edmunds recommends sticking with the GLA 250 over the AMG variant for value.

owner_reviews.summary: Very small KBB sample (5 reviews) skews overwhelmingly positive, with owners citing comfort, build quality and performance; some flag a learning curve on MBUX controls. Edmunds 2026 owner-review sample is not yet meaningfully populated.

models.generation_context: First-generation (X247), launched for 2020 MY; mid-cycle facelift in 2024 added 48V mild-hybrid I4. The 2026 MY is the final year of the X247 before the second-generation (X244) arrives globally (announced Dec 2025); for the US 2026 lineup remains the X247 with the M260 mild-hybrid I4.
models.model_summary: The 2026 GLB is Mercedes-Benz's compact luxury SUV — boxier and more upright than the GLA hatchback-style crossover, sharing the MFA2 platform but designed around interior space and an available third row that makes it the only 7-seat option in this size class. The US lineup for 2026 is two trims, both powered by the same 221-hp 2.0L turbo I4 with 48V mild-hybrid assist and an 8-speed dual-clutch transmission, distinguished only by FWD versus 4MATIC AWD. The AMG GLB 35 performance variant offered in prior years is not part of the US 2026 catalog on mbusa.com.

reliability.summary: JD Power has not yet published a VDS score for the 2026 GLB. KBB owner data (sample of 25) reports an above-average 4.4/5 reliability rating from current-MY owners, suggesting the well-established X247 platform's reliability profile continues to be acceptable, but no formal long-term study covers the 2026 specifically.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power has not published a 2026 APEAL score for the GLB. KBB owner sentiment (n=25) is largely positive with an overall 4.1/5 rating and 80% of owners recommending the vehicle; comfort and quality rate strongest, value rates weakest.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently highlight the GLB's class-leading interior space — particularly its available third row, the only one in the subcompact-luxury bracket — its strong outward visibility, quick steering and quiet, planted highway ride. Common criticisms center on a noticeable turbo lag off the line, hard interior plastics in some lower-touch areas, and a starting price that's above key rivals once destination is added. Edmunds rates it 7.8/10 and notes a 6.5-second 0-60 in their independent test (versus Mercedes' 6.9-second claim).

owner_reviews.summary: KBB owners praise comfort, build quality (4.5/5) and reliability (4.4/5); value/cost is the weakest dimension. 80% recommend, with 64% giving it a perfect 5-star rating overall. Edmunds owner data for the 2026 MY is not yet aggregated.

models.generation_context: Second-generation X254, introduced for 2023 model year and refreshed for 2026 with expanded electrification (48V mild-hybrid across ICE line and two plug-in hybrid variants); built on Mercedes' MRA II rear-drive architecture
models.model_summary: The 2026 Mercedes-Benz GLC SUV is the compact luxury crossover anchor of the brand's US lineup, offered in both 48V mild-hybrid ICE form (GLC 300, GLC 300 4MATIC, AMG GLC 43) and plug-in hybrid form (GLC 350e 4MATIC and AMG GLC 63 S E Performance). All trims share Mercedes' MBUX infotainment with 11.9-inch portrait touchscreen, 12.3-inch driver display, and the second-generation 2.0L M254/M139 turbocharged inline-four engine family. The PHEV trims add a 24-kWh (350e) or 6.1-kWh (63 S) battery for substantial (350e) or supplementary (AMG) electric driving.

reliability.summary: Consumer Reports gives the current-generation GLC mid-pack predicted reliability; JD Power has not published a current-MY VDS score for the 2026 GLC. The X254 generation has had mixed early-ownership feedback around the MBUX infotainment system and 48V mild-hybrid starter-generator.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has NOT yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (the US APEAL Study typically releases in July). professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently praise the GLC for its hushed cabin, plush ride and high-quality interior, calling out the 11.9-inch portrait MBUX touchscreen and the standard 48V mild-hybrid system that smooths low-speed acceleration. Common criticisms include a touch-heavy control interface that buries climate functions, modest cargo space versus rivals like the BMW X3 and Audi Q5, and 0-60 times in the base GLC 300 that lag the segment despite the mild-hybrid boost. The AMG GLC 63 S E Performance earns acclaim for raw 671-hp performance but draws criticism for compromised everyday usability and steep $86,750 starting price.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise the GLC's quiet, premium-feeling cabin, ride comfort and styling; common complaints center on MBUX touchscreen complexity, frequent reliance on touch controls for routine functions, and software-related quirks with the 48V mild-hybrid system at start-up.

models.model_summary: The 2026 Mercedes-Benz GLC Coupe is the fastback-roofline derivative of the GLC SUV, offered exclusively as 4MATIC all-wheel drive in the US for 2026. The lineup spans a 255-hp 48V mild-hybrid GLC 300 4MATIC Coupe base, the 416-hp AMG GLC 43 (also a 48V mild hybrid), and the 671-hp AMG GLC 63 S E Performance plug-in hybrid flagship. The coupe variant trades cargo room and rear headroom versus the GLC SUV for a more dramatic profile and slightly sharper steering tuning.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the GLC Coupe's polished ride, upscale MBUX interior with the standard 11.9-inch portrait touchscreen, and strong AMG performance variants, particularly the GLC 63 S E Performance's brutal 671-hp PHEV output. Common criticisms include reduced rear headroom and a smaller cargo opening versus the GLC SUV, a steep price walk to the AMG trims, and the surprisingly short EV-only range of the GLC 63's 6.1 kWh battery for a car billed as a plug-in hybrid.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner-review sample sizes for the 2026 GLC Coupe are still small. Early KBB ownership data shows a 5-star quality rating with 67% recommending the vehicle; common praise focuses on luxury feel and AMG performance, while complaints concern reduced rear-seat headroom and tight cargo opening compared to the GLC SUV.

models.model_summary: The 2026 Mercedes-Benz GLE SUV is a midsize five-passenger luxury SUV offered in a broad range of powertrains: a 2.0L turbo-four (GLE 350), a 3.0L turbo inline-six (GLE 450), a 3.0L turbo I6 plug-in hybrid (GLE 450e), a 4.0L biturbo V8 (GLE 580), and two AMG variants (GLE 53 and GLE 63 S). All non-PHEV gasoline engines pair with an integrated starter-generator 48V mild-hybrid system that adds up to 23 hp on demand. A third-row jump seat is optional on most trims (3+2 seating) but officially a 5-seater.

reliability.summary: Consumer Reports lists predicted reliability for the GLE-Class at average. JD Power 2025 VDS for the 2022-MY GLE was below segment average; no 2026-specific VDS yet. Recent GLE generations have seen recalls related to electrical systems, sunroof and 48V mild-hybrid components.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the 2026 GLE for its smooth and powerful inline-six and V8 engines, refined ride quality (particularly with the optional Airmatic suspension), upscale and well-built interior, and the strong list of standard driver-assist technology. Common criticisms include the touch-capacitive steering-wheel controls (now improved over earlier MBUX iterations but still divisive), a relatively cramped optional third-row seat, and pricing that rises quickly with options. The 450e PHEV is singled out for an exceptional all-electric range that beats most rivals.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners commonly praise the GLE's comfortable ride, quiet cabin, powerful engines and luxurious interior. Recurring complaints involve the complexity of the MBUX infotainment system, the cost of optional packages, and several reports of electronics glitches.

models.model_summary: The 2026 Mercedes-Benz GLE Coupe is the sloping-roofline 'coupe-SUV' counterpart to the GLE SUV, sharing the W167/C167 platform but trading some headroom and cargo capacity for a more aggressive fastback profile. The 2026 US lineup is narrower than the GLE SUV: it skips the 2.0L turbo-four base, the GLE 580 V8 and the 450e PHEV, starting instead with the 375-hp GLE 450 4MATIC inline-six (all AWD), then stepping up to the AMG GLE 53 4MATIC+ (429 hp turbo I6) and the range-topping AMG GLE 63 S 4MATIC+ (603 hp biturbo V8). All powertrains pair their gasoline engine with an EQ Boost 48V mild-hybrid integrated starter-generator.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the 2026 GLE Coupe for its strong inline-six and V8 engines, refined ride (particularly with the optional Airmatic air suspension on the 450 and standard on AMG variants), upscale interior and well-integrated MBUX infotainment. Critics consistently note that the coupe roofline costs rear headroom and cargo volume versus the regular GLE SUV without delivering meaningful driving-dynamic gains, and that the AMG GLE 63 S, while staggeringly quick, is among the thirstiest vehicles in its class. Pricing climbs steeply once options are added.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise the GLE Coupe's powerful engines, quiet refined cabin and distinctive styling. Recurring complaints include compromised rear-seat headroom for taller passengers, reduced cargo practicality versus the GLE SUV, and the cost of optional packages required to make a base trim feel fully equipped.

models.model_summary: The 2026 GLS is Mercedes-Benz's full-size, three-row flagship SUV — the 'S-Class of SUVs' — with seating for seven and a roster of 48V mild-hybrid powertrains spanning a 375-hp turbo I6 (GLS 450), a 510-hp twin-turbo V8 (GLS 580) and a hand-built 603-hp AMG-tuned twin-turbo V8 (AMG GLS 63). All trims come standard with 4MATIC all-wheel drive, the 9G-TRONIC nine-speed automatic, AIRMATIC air suspension, and a 12.3-inch driver display paired with a 12.3-inch MBUX touchscreen.

reliability.summary: JD Power has not published a 2026 VDS score for the GLS; Consumer Reports has historically rated the GLS-Class around average for predicted reliability, with electronics and infotainment issues being the most commonly reported complaints on the X167 generation.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently praise the GLS for its limousine-like ride quality on AIRMATIC air suspension, hushed cabin, refined powertrains and genuine three-row capability with usable adult-size second-row space. Common criticisms include third-row entry/egress that lags some competitors, a high starting price that climbs rapidly with options, and a portly curb weight that blunts handling agility. The AMG GLS 63 is singled out for its remarkable speed-versus-size combination but called overkill for most buyers.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners cite the GLS's plush ride, quiet cabin, and powertrain refinement as primary strengths; recurring complaints involve MBUX infotainment glitches, the complexity of options pricing, and high running costs (premium fuel, tires, brakes).

models.generation_context: Mercedes-Maybach GLS 600 4MATIC, the ultra-luxury Maybach derivative of the X167-generation GLS-Class; introduced for 2021 model year and refreshed for 2024+ with updated MBUX, revised front fascia, and additional Manufaktur personalization options
models.model_summary: The Mercedes-Maybach GLS 600 4MATIC is the chauffeured-flagship SUV of the Mercedes-Maybach sub-brand. Built on the GLS chassis but redesigned around its 2nd-row passengers, it features standard executive captain's chairs with massage and ventilation, electric leg rests, double-glazed acoustic glass, 27-speaker Burmester High-End 3D surround sound, and Maybach-exclusive trim, paint and badging. A 4.0-liter biturbo V8 (M177) with 48V mild-hybrid EQ Boost produces 550 hp / 538 lb-ft and routes through a 9-speed automatic and 4MATIC AWD with E-Active Body Control air suspension.

reliability.summary: JD Power 2026 US VDS rates the mercedes-benz brand at 235 PP100 (lower is better) — worse than the 204 industry average and worse than the 217 premium-segment average. Consumer Reports has not published a 2026 model-specific score; the model inherits the Audi/BMW/Mercedes brand reliability picture (below average (GLS based), est. 2/5). Sourced from the published CR 2026 Brand Report Card and JD Power 2026 VDS brand ranking.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has NOT yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (the US APEAL Study typically releases in July). professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently rank the Maybach GLS 600 as one of the most opulent SUVs on sale, praising its near-silent cabin, executive rear-seat experience (heated/cooled/massaging captain's chairs with calf rests), 27-speaker Burmester 3D audio and standard E-Active Body Control air suspension. Common criticisms include a polarizing two-tone exterior, fuel economy in the mid-teens, and a price tag that overlaps Bentley Bentayga and Rolls-Royce Cullinan territory. The hand-finished Manufaktur interior and dedicated Maybach concierge service are repeatedly called the segment's high-water mark.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner-review sample sizes on Edmunds and KBB for the Maybach GLS are too small to report a meaningful star rating; the vehicle is a low-volume, chauffeur-oriented flagship. Anecdotal owner feedback praises the rear-seat experience and ride quality while flagging cost of ownership and dealer service availability outside major metros.

models.model_summary: The 2026 G-Class is Mercedes' body-on-frame, three-locking-differential luxury off-road SUV available in three powertrain configurations: the G 550 with a 443-hp 3.0L turbocharged inline-6 mild hybrid (the new base ICE), the all-electric G 580 with EQ Technology featuring four wheel-side electric motors producing 579 hp and 859 lb-ft of torque, and the Mercedes-AMG G 63 with a 577-hp handcrafted 4.0L V8 biturbo with mild-hybrid assist. All variants retain the traditional ladder-frame G-Wagon construction, full-time 4WD with two-speed transfer case and low range, and the three sequentially lockable differentials that define the G's off-road capability.

reliability.summary: JD Power 2026 VDS and Consumer Reports predicted reliability data are not published for the refreshed G-Class with new inline-6 + MHEV powertrain or the all-new electric G 580. Historical G-Class reliability data has been limited due to low sales volume. KBB consumer ratings indicate above-average owner-perceived reliability (4.4 / 5).

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power APEAL score is not published for the 2026 G-Class. KBB owner ratings show high overall satisfaction (4.7 / 5) — typical for the G-Class as an aspirational status vehicle.

professional_reviews.summary: Critics universally acknowledge the 2026 G-Class as one of the most capable off-road production SUVs ever made and a singular status symbol, with all three powertrains delivering brisk on-road acceleration. Edmunds rates it 5.4/10, noting that despite its commanding presence the cabin is less spacious than its footprint suggests, on-road handling remains trucky, and ingress/egress is awkward. Reviewers praise the electric G 580 for retaining genuine off-road capability — including G-Turn (tank-turn) functionality and 33.5 inches of fording depth — while admitting the ~239-mile EPA range is short for a six-figure EV.

owner_reviews.summary: KBB consumer reviews give the G-Class an overall 4.7/5 with reliability at 4.4/5. Common praise centers on iconic styling, build quality, and unmatched road presence; complaints focus on fuel economy, tight rear-seat ingress and the harsh ride from solid-axle suspension. Edmunds 2026 owner-review sample is not yet populated.

models.model_summary: The 2026 Mercedes-Benz EQE Sedan is a midsize luxury electric sedan that targets the BMW i5 and Audi A6 e-tron. For 2026 Mercedes RENAMED the rear- and all-wheel-drive trims from EQE 350+/350 4MATIC to EQE 320+/320 4MATIC, even though horsepower actually increased (now 315 hp). The lineup is EQE 320+ (RWD, 308 mi EPA range), EQE 320 4MATIC (AWD, 267 mi), and the high-performance AMG EQE 4MATIC+ (617 hp / 677 with launch control, 220 mi). All use a 96 kWh (90.6 kWh usable) lithium-ion battery, 170 kW CCS DC fast charging, and a standard panoramic roof.

reliability.summary: Consumer Reports projects the 2026 EQE as less reliable than average new cars, driven by Mercedes' below-average brand reliability and historical issues with Mercedes EV electronics. JD Power has not yet published a 2026-MY VDS score for the EQE Sedan.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has NOT yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (the US APEAL Study typically releases in July). No 2026 model-specific APEAL score is available for this model. The 2025 APEAL score may be applicable as carryover but has not been adopted here pending the 2026 release.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the EQE Sedan's tight build quality, lavish materials, supremely comfortable front seats and isolating ride, calling it the right choice for buyers who prefer cosseting luxury over sporting dynamics. Criticisms center on flat rear seat cushions with an awkward backrest angle, glare-prone Hyperscreen, and the fact that the 308-mile range and acceleration no longer stand out against newer EV competitors. The 2026 renaming (350+/350 4MATIC to 320+/320 4MATIC) has been widely flagged as confusing because horsepower actually went up despite the lower trim number.

owner_reviews.summary: Not enough 2026-MY owner reviews aggregated yet at Edmunds or KBB. Earlier MY EQE owners commonly praise ride comfort and cabin quietness while complaining about software glitches and tight rear headroom.

models.model_summary: The 2026 Mercedes-Benz EQE SUV is an all-electric midsize luxury SUV positioned between the smaller EQB and the larger EQS SUV. For 2026 Mercedes renamed the SUV trims: the prior 'EQE 350' nomenclature is gone — the lineup now consists of the rear-drive EQE 320+ (315 hp, 302-mile EPA range), the dual-motor EQE 320 4MATIC (315 hp combined, 253 miles), and the high-performance AMG EQE 4MATIC+ SUV (617 hp / 677 hp with Dynamic Plus, 230 miles). reliability.summary: Consumer Reports expects the EQE SUV to be less reliable than the average new vehicle, citing Mercedes' below-average brand reliability and reported issues with onboard electronics and the high-voltage battery management system (subject of a 2024 NHTSA recall). No JD Power VDS score is published for the 2026 model year.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has NOT yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (the US APEAL Study typically releases in July). No 2026 model-specific APEAL score is available for this model. The 2025 APEAL score may be applicable as carryover but has not been adopted here pending the 2026 release.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently describe the 2026 EQE SUV as quiet, comfortable and richly trimmed, with real-world range that comfortably beats the EPA label — Edmunds drove the EQE 320+ 349 miles in its range test, 47 miles above EPA. Common criticisms target the MBUX touchscreen and app for being over-complex and occasionally laggy, plus mushy brake feel from the heavy regen blending. The car is widely seen as more refined cruiser than enthusiast pick, with the AMG variant offering supercar acceleration but at the cost of range.

owner_reviews.summary: 2026-specific owner-review samples are still small. Carryover sentiment from the 2025 EQE SUV (which is essentially the same vehicle with different trim names) is mixed: praise for quiet, smooth ride and luxurious cabin; complaints center on mushy brake pedal feel, the over-complex MBUX interface, and the Mercedes me Connect app.

models.model_summary: The 2026 Mercedes-Benz EQS Sedan is the brand's full-size flagship electric four-door, sitting alongside (and electrically substituting for) the S-Class. The lineup spans four trims — single-motor RWD EQS 450+, dual-motor AWD EQS 450 4MATIC, the 516-hp dual-motor EQS 580 4MATIC, and the 649-hp (751 hp with Dynamic Plus) AMG EQS 53 4MATIC+ — all sharing a 108.4-kWh usable lithium-ion battery, 200-kW peak DC fast charging via CCS1, and the standard 12.8-inch portrait MBUX touchscreen (with the optional 56-inch curved Hyperscreen spanning the dash). EPA range tops out at roughly 390 miles on the rear-drive 450+.

reliability.summary: Consumer Reports has rated EQS predicted reliability around average (3/5) based on cumulative survey data since 2022. JD Power has not published a current-MY VDS score for the EQS Sedan; earlier MYs reported some software/MBUX glitches and rear-axle steering issues but no widespread powertrain reliability concerns.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power has not published a 2026 APEAL score specific to the EQS Sedan. In prior APEAL studies, full-size luxury EVs (including the EQS) scored well above segment average for design and tech appeal but below average for ease of use.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers consistently praise the EQS Sedan's whisper-quiet cabin, supple air-spring ride, long EPA range, and the technological theater of the optional MBUX Hyperscreen. Common criticisms are the polarizing 'jelly bean' silhouette versus the S-Class's traditional three-box shape, a hatchback opening that doesn't fold flat (trunk pass-through is via rear seats), and a 200-kW DC fast-charging peak that lags newer 800-V rivals like the Lucid Air and Porsche Taycan. The AMG EQS 53 is fast in a straight line but its weight (5,600+ lb) blunts handling against ICE AMG sedans.

owner_reviews.summary: Owners praise the EQS Sedan's serene ride, quiet cabin, and effortless powertrain refinement; common complaints across MYs include MBUX software quirks, polarizing exterior styling, and the trunk's awkward shape under the fastback liftgate. Sample sizes for the 2026 MY remain very small as of May 2026.

notable_other: Optional 56-inch MBUX Hyperscreen (three displays under a single curved glass)
Standard air suspension with adaptive damping (AIRMATIC)
Standard rear-axle steering (up to 4.5 degrees; 10 degrees with optional package)
11 kW onboard AC charging (US spec capped at 9.6 kW on most circuits)
200 kW DC fast charging via CCS1 — 10-80% in ~31 minutes on 200+ kW charger
EPA range of 390 mi is the longest in the EQS Sedan lineup
Frunk: none (HVAC components occupy front compartment)
Drag coefficient 0.20 Cd — among the lowest of any production car

trims.notes: Rear-wheel-drive single-motor base trim. Carries the 2024 MY battery upgrade (108.4 kWh usable) forward into 2026. 200 kW DC fast charging via CCS1 plug; native NACS is not offered on the EQS Sedan for 2026 MY (Mercedes' NACS transition begins with the new MMA-platform CLA and EQE/EQS successors). NHTSA and IIHS have not published current crash-test ratings for the EQS Sedan as of May 2026 — set to null.

Adds a second front motor for 4MATIC AWD; battery, charging speed, dimensions, and feature content are otherwise identical to the EQS 450+. Front motor remains permanently engaged but is electronically modulated for efficiency.

notable_other: MBUX Hyperscreen (56-inch curved glass with three displays) is standard
Standard Nappa leather upholstery (synthetic on lower trims)
Standard rear-axle steering with 10-degree angle (optional on 450 trims)
Standard active ambient lighting (64-color)
Optional Executive Rear Package with reclining rear seats and rear MBUX tablet

trims.notes: Same 108.4 kWh usable battery and 200 kW DC fast-charging cap as the 450 trims. The major upgrades are powertrain output, standard Hyperscreen, standard Nappa leather, and standard 10-degree rear-axle steering — items that are optional or unavailable on the 450 trims.

notable_other: AMG-specific dual motors with stronger windings — 649 hp / 700 lb-ft standard, 751 hp / 752 lb-ft with optional AMG Dynamic Plus package (Race Start)
AMG-tuned AIRMATIC air suspension with active anti-roll
Standard MBUX Hyperscreen (56-inch curved glass)
Top speed raised to 155 mph (electronically limited; 130 mph on other trims)
AMG-specific exterior styling (front fascia, rear diffuser, badging) and 21" forged wheels
AMG Sound Experience (synthesized exterior + interior sound profiles)
Rear-axle steering up to 9 degrees
Standard AMG performance seats with Nappa leather

trims.notes: AMG performance variant of the EQS Sedan, treated here as a trim within the EQS Sedan model per the project's rule that AMG step-up trims with the same EV powertrain type sit in one model line. Top speed bumps to 155 mph (vs 130 mph on non-AMG trims). The 'AMG Dynamic Plus' package is a paid option that raises peak output from 649 hp to 751 hp via launch-control software. Battery, charging hardware, and platform are shared with the rest of the EQS Sedan range.

models.notes: EQS Sedan continues into 2026 MY on the EVA1 platform with the 2024 MY battery upgrade carried forward (108.4 kWh usable / 118 kWh nominal). All trims use the CCS1 plug with 200 kW peak DC fast charging — the EQS Sedan does NOT receive native NACS for 2026 MY (Mercedes' NACS rollout begins with the new MMA-platform CLA and the next-generation EQE/EQS due later). NHTSA and IIHS have not published current crash-test ratings for the EQS Sedan as of May 2026 — safety.nhtsa_overall_rating and safety.iihs_top_safety_pick are null. Standard ADAS suite (AEB, lane keep, ACC, blind spot, etc.) is included on all trims via Mercedes' Driver Assistance package as standard equipment. The AMG EQS 53 is included as a trim within EQS Sedan rather than as a separate model, per the project rule for AMG EV step-ups in a shared powertrain line.

models.generation_context: First generation EQS SUV (X296), introduced for 2023 MY; refreshed for 2026 with updated styling (new upright grille treatment), new EQS 400 4MATIC and EQS 550 4MATIC powertrains replacing the prior EQS 450/580 lineup, and a larger 118 kWh (108.4 kWh usable) battery pack
models.model_summary: The 2026 Mercedes-Benz EQS SUV is a refreshed three-row, all-electric flagship SUV available in 5- or 7-seat configurations. The 2026 model year drops the previous EQS 450/580 trims and introduces two new dual-motor 4MATIC variants — the EQS 400 (~355 hp) and the higher-output EQS 550 (~536 hp) — both powered by a 118 kWh (108.4 kWh usable) lithium-ion battery with 200 kW peak DC fast charging. The refresh also brings revised front-end styling with a more traditional upright grille, replacing the prior solid black panel.

reliability.summary: JD Power 2026 US VDS rates the mercedes-benz brand at 235 PP100 (lower is better) — worse than the 204 industry average and worse than the 217 premium-segment average. Consumer Reports has not published a 2026 model-specific score; the model inherits the Audi/BMW/Mercedes brand reliability picture (below average (brand fallback), est. 2/5). Sourced from the published CR 2026 Brand Report Card and JD Power 2026 VDS brand ranking.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has NOT yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (the US APEAL Study typically releases in July). No 2026 model-specific APEAL score is available for this model. The 2025 APEAL score may be applicable as carryover but has not been adopted here pending the 2026 release.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers praise the EQS SUV for its quiet, plush ride, opulent cabin with the available Hyperscreen, and genuine three-row usability. The 2026 refresh's new upright grille and revised powertrains were welcomed, though the larger battery's range gains are modest. Common criticisms include the SUV's substantial curb weight (roughly 6,000 lb), slower DC fast-charge peak (200 kW) versus newer 800V rivals, and a steep starting price.

owner_reviews.summary: Refreshed 2026 model year is too new for meaningful owner-review aggregation; prior-MY EQS SUV owners broadly praise comfort and cabin quietness while citing range realism and charging speed as drawbacks.

Standard 5-seat 2-row configuration; optional power-folding 3rd row (7-seat capacity)

trims.notes: Base trim of refreshed 2026 EQS SUV lineup; replaces prior EQS 450 4MATIC. The 2026 refresh adds a redesigned upright grille treatment and the larger 118 kWh (108.4 kWh usable) battery pack. NHTSA and IIHS have not crash-tested the refreshed 2026 EQS SUV; ratings null. Third-row seat is a stand-alone option; standard configuration is 5-seat / 2-row. Towing capacity 3,500 lb with the optional trailer hitch.

Optional 3rd row power-folding bench for 7-passenger capacity. Curb weight increases over EQS 400 due to additional standard equipment and higher-output rear motor. models.notes: 2026 model year is a mid-cycle refresh of the X296 EQS SUV: new upright illuminated grille replaces the prior solid black panel face, and the powertrain lineup is renumbered — EQS 400 4MATIC and EQS 550 4MATIC supersede the EQS 450 4MATIC and EQS 580 4MATIC respectively. Both 2026 trims use a larger 118 kWh (108.4 kWh usable) battery pack. DC fast charging remains at a 200 kW peak via CCS1 (not NACS — the EQS SUV did not adopt the NACS plug in this refresh; that transition is reserved for newer Mercedes EVs on the MMA/MB.EA platforms). 5-seat 2-row is standard; 7-seat with power-folding 3rd row is a stand-alone option on both trims. NHTSA and IIHS have not rated the refreshed 2026 EQS SUV — both fields null. fuel_economy MPG fields populated with EPA MPGe per project rules for EV trims; fuel_tank_gal null. EPA annual fuel cost and combined MPGe estimated pending live fueleconomy.gov listing for refreshed 2026 trims; verify on publish.

models.generation_context: First-generation Mercedes-Maybach EQS SUV (X296), introduced for 2024 model year as the brand's flagship full-electric Maybach; built on the EVA2 electric SUV platform shared with the Mercedes-Benz EQS SUV and continued largely unchanged for 2026
models.model_summary: The 2026 Mercedes-Maybach EQS 680 SUV is the most opulent battery-electric SUV in the Mercedes portfolio and the only fully-electric Maybach sold in the US. A single dual-motor 4MATIC trim produces 649 hp combined and delivers an EPA-rated 300 miles of range from its 118 kWh (108.4 kWh usable) battery. Maybach exclusives include the Executive Rear Seat package, a 4-seat lounge interior option, two-tone paintwork, Burmester High-End 4D Surround Sound, and standard 22-inch Maybach forged wheels.

reliability.summary: JD Power 2026 US VDS rates the mercedes-benz brand at 235 PP100 (lower is better) — worse than the 204 industry average and worse than the 217 premium-segment average. Consumer Reports has not published a 2026 model-specific score; the model inherits the Audi/BMW/Mercedes brand reliability picture (below average (EQS SUV based), est. 2/5). Sourced from the published CR 2026 Brand Report Card and JD Power 2026 VDS brand ranking.

customer_satisfaction.summary: JD Power 2026 APEAL Study has NOT yet been published as of 2026-05-15 (the US APEAL Study typically releases in July). No 2026 model-specific APEAL score is available for this model. The 2025 APEAL score may be applicable as carryover but has not been adopted here pending the 2026 release.

professional_reviews.summary: Reviewers describe the Maybach EQS SUV as a serenely quiet, ultra-plush three-row electric flagship whose reclining executive rear seats, calf massagers and Burmester 4D system redefine the rear-seat experience among EV SUVs. Critics note that the 300-mile EPA range trails comparable luxury rivals (Cadillac Escalade IQ, Rolls-Royce Spectre), that the chrome-heavy styling polarizes, and that the third row is best treated as occasional-use given the lounge-oriented second row. Most reviews praise the rear-wheel steering, four-chamber AIRMATIC air suspension and near-silent cabin.

owner_reviews.summary: Owner-review sample sizes for the Maybach EQS SUV remain too small for meaningful aggregation on Edmunds or KBB.

trims.notes: Sole 2026 Maybach EQS SUV trim — therefore is_base_trim is true and delta_from_base is null. NHTSA and IIHS have not crash-tested the Maybach EQS SUV; the related Mercedes-Benz EQS SUV likewise lacks current ratings. Maximum cargo with second and third rows folded is ~74 cu ft; when the 4-seat lounge configuration is selected, second-row seats do not fold flat, reducing maximum cargo. The 4-seat configuration is the headline Maybach layout; a 5-seat configuration with a standard second-row bench (deleting the third row) is available at no extra cost. DC fast-charge peak is 200 kW via CCS1; NACS adapter availability is dealer-installed. Complimentary maintenance for 5 years is part of the Maybach Concierge program.

models.notes: Mercedes-Maybach EQS SUV is the Maybach sub-brand's only fully-electric model and is sold in the US in a single 'EQS 680 4MATIC' trim. Built alongside the Mercedes-Benz EQS SUV at the Tuscaloosa, Alabama plant. The Maybach EQS SUV continues for 2026 without significant content changes from its 2024 US debut; pricing remains $180,900 plus $1,295 destination. Despite the 'EQS 680' naming, the dual-motor powertrain produces 649 hp combined (rounded to '680' per Maybach's continental nomenclature, not a direct horsepower figure). EPA confirms 84 MPGe combined and 300 miles of range on fueleconomy.gov.
